# Toolr docstring conventions

<!-- generated by `cargo xtask build-skill-refs`; do not edit by hand -->

Toolr parses Google-style docstrings on every command and group. The parser lives in `crates/toolr-core/src/docstrings.rs` and drives the `--help` output your users see.
This reference is generated from the same `KNOWN_SECTION_HEADERS` table the parser reads at runtime, so the conventions documented here are the conventions the parser recognises — they cannot drift.

## Structure

- The **first non-empty line** of the docstring becomes the command's short help — the one-liner shown in the parent group's `--help` listing.
- Body text before the first section header becomes the long description shown on `<command> --help`.
- An `Args:` section (also accepts `Arguments:` / `Parameters:`, case-insensitive) provides per-parameter help in the form `name: description`.
- An `Examples:` section is rendered in the long help via [termimad](https://github.com/Canop/termimad); fenced code blocks and `>>>` doctest blocks both work.
- Section detection is **case-insensitive**: `Args:`, `ARGS:`, and `args:` are all equivalent.

## Recognised section headers

Each header below is matched against the start of a trimmed, lowercased line. Headers ending in `:` match the standalone section form (`Args:`); headers ending in a space match the inline form (`Attr foo:`).

| Category | Recognised headers |
| --- | --- |
| `args` | `args:`, `arguments:`, `parameters:` |
| `attr` | `attr <name>`, `attribute <name>` |
| `attributes` | `attributes:`, `attrs:` |
| `deprecated` | `deprecated:` |
| `examples` | `example:`, `examples:` |
| `notes` | `note:`, `notes:` |
| `raises` | `except:`, `raise:`, `raises:` |
| `references` | `references:`, `refs:` |
| `returns` | `return:`, `returns:` |
| `see_also` | `see also:`, `see:` |
| `todo` | `todo:` |
| `version_added` | `version added:` |
| `version_changed` | `version changed:` |
| `warnings` | `warning:`, `warnings:` |
| `yields` | `yield:`, `yields:` |

Categories `returns`, `yields`, `raises`, `attributes`, and `attr` are detected by the parser but currently ignored when rendering help. They are listed above so you know they will not raise "unknown section" errors; use them freely.
